Analisis exploratorio

load("datos/listings.RData")
ls()
## [1] "listings"
library(nortest)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(cluster)
library(mclust)
## Package 'mclust' version 6.1.2
## Type 'citation("mclust")' for citing this R package in publications.
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'mclust'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     count
library(NbClust)
library(factoextra)
## Welcome! Want to learn more? See two factoextra-related books at https://goo.gl/ve3WBa
library(hopkins)
library(GGally)
colnames(listings)

dim(listings)
## [1] 171748     80
head(listings)
tipos_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(listings),
  Tipo = sapply(listings, class),
  row.names = NULL
)
View(tipos_df)
summary(listings)

##  Min.   : 0.00000                            Min.   :  0.010  
##  1st Qu.: 0.00000                            1st Qu.:  0.190  
##  Median : 0.00000                            Median :  0.700

##                                              NA's   :40287    
##      city          
##  Length:171748     
##  Class :character  
##  Mode  :character  
##                    
##                    
##                    
## 
numericas <- c("accommodates","bathrooms","bedrooms","beds","host_response_rate","host_acceptance_rate","calculated_host_listings_count","number_of_reviews","reviews_per_month","review_scores_rating","minimum_nights","availability_365"
)

sapply(listings[numericas], class)
##                   accommodates                      bathrooms 
##                      "integer"                      "numeric" 
##                       bedrooms                           beds 
##                      "integer"                      "integer" 
##             host_response_rate           host_acceptance_rate 
##                    "character"                    "character" 
## calculated_host_listings_count              number_of_reviews 
##                      "integer"                      "integer" 
##              reviews_per_month           review_scores_rating 
##                      "numeric"                      "numeric" 
##                 minimum_nights               availability_365 
##                      "integer"                      "integer"
listings$host_response_rate <- gsub("%", "", listings$host_response_rate)
listings$host_response_rate[listings$host_response_rate == "N/A"] <- NA
listings$host_response_rate <- as.numeric(listings$host_response_rate)

listings$host_acceptance_rate <- gsub("%", "", listings$host_acceptance_rate)
listings$host_acceptance_rate[listings$host_acceptance_rate == "N/A"] <- NA
listings$host_acceptance_rate <- as.numeric(listings$host_acceptance_rate)

listings$price <- as.numeric(gsub("[$,]", "", listings$price))


listings <- listings %>% filter(!is.na(price))
colSums(is.na(listings[numericas]))
##                   accommodates                      bathrooms 
##                              0                             14 
##                       bedrooms                           beds 
##                            121                             80 
##             host_response_rate           host_acceptance_rate 
##                           5376                           4301 
## calculated_host_listings_count              number_of_reviews 
##                              0                              0 
##              reviews_per_month           review_scores_rating 
##                          13413                          13413 
##                 minimum_nights               availability_365 
##                              0                              0
colSums(is.na(listings[numericas])) / nrow(listings)
##                   accommodates                      bathrooms 
##                   0.0000000000                   0.0001836162 
##                       bedrooms                           beds 
##                   0.0015869685                   0.0010492354 
##             host_response_rate           host_acceptance_rate 
##                   0.0705086168                   0.0564095166 
## calculated_host_listings_count              number_of_reviews 
##                   0.0000000000                   0.0000000000 
##              reviews_per_month           review_scores_rating 
##                   0.1759174252                   0.1759174252 
##                 minimum_nights               availability_365 
##                   0.0000000000                   0.0000000000
head(listings$bathrooms_text)
## [1] "1 bath"        "1 bath"        "1 bath"        "2 baths"      
## [5] "1 bath"        "1 shared bath"
listings$bathrooms_num <- as.numeric(sub("[^0-9.].*", "",listings$bathrooms_text))
listings$bathrooms_num[grepl("Half", listings$bathrooms_text, ignore.case = TRUE)] <- 0.5

sum(is.na(listings$bathrooms_num))
## [1] 229
listings$bathrooms_num[is.na(listings$bathrooms_num)] <- 
median(listings$bathrooms_num, na.rm = TRUE)
listings$bathroom_type <- ifelse(
  grepl("shared", listings$bathrooms_text, ignore.case = TRUE),
  "shared",
  "private"
)

listings$bathroom_type <- as.factor(listings$bathroom_type)
summary(listings$bathrooms_num)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   0.000   1.000   1.000   1.612   2.000  20.000
table(listings$bathroom_type)
## 
## private  shared 
##   71654    4592
listings$bathrooms_text <- NULL
listings$bathrooms <- NULL
listings$bedrooms[is.na(listings$bedrooms)] <- median(listings$bedrooms, na.rm = TRUE)

listings$beds[is.na(listings$beds)] <- 
median(listings$beds, na.rm = TRUE)

listings$host_response_missing <- ifelse(is.na(listings$host_response_rate), 1, 0)
listings$host_response_rate[is.na(listings$host_response_rate)] <- 
mean(listings$host_response_rate, na.rm = TRUE)

listings$host_acceptance_missing <- ifelse(is.na(listings$host_acceptance_rate), 1, 0)
listings$host_acceptance_rate[is.na(listings$host_acceptance_rate)] <- 
mean(listings$host_acceptance_rate, na.rm = TRUE)

listings$reviews_per_month[is.na(listings$reviews_per_month)] <- 0

listings$has_reviews <- ifelse(is.na(listings$review_scores_rating), 0, 1)
listings$review_scores_rating[is.na(listings$review_scores_rating)] <- 
mean(listings$review_scores_rating, na.rm = TRUE)
numericas_final <- c(
"price",
"accommodates",
"bathrooms_num",
"bedrooms",
"beds",
"host_response_rate",
"host_acceptance_rate",
"calculated_host_listings_count",
"number_of_reviews",
"reviews_per_month",
"review_scores_rating",
"minimum_nights",
"availability_365"
)

colSums(is.na(listings[, numericas_final]))
##                          price                   accommodates 
##                              0                              0 
##                  bathrooms_num                       bedrooms 
##                              0                              0 
##                           beds             host_response_rate 
##                              0                              0 
##           host_acceptance_rate calculated_host_listings_count 
##                              0                              0 
##              number_of_reviews              reviews_per_month 
##                              0                              0 
##           review_scores_rating                 minimum_nights 
##                              0                              0 
##               availability_365 
##                              0

Medidas de tendencia central

mean(listings$accommodates, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 4.841369
median(listings$accommodates, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 4
min(listings$accommodates, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 1
max(listings$accommodates, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 16
quantile(listings$accommodates, na.rm = TRUE)
##   0%  25%  50%  75% 100% 
##    1    2    4    6   16
hist(listings$accommodates,
     main = "Histograma de Accommodates",
     xlab = "Accommodates",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "white")

boxplot(listings$accommodates,
        main = "Boxplot de Accommodates",
        col = "lightgreen")

lillie.test(sample(listings$accommodates,
                   min(5000, length(listings$accommodates))))
## 
##  Lilliefors (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) normality test
## 
## data:  sample(listings$accommodates, min(5000, length(listings$accommodates)))
## D = 0.20719, p-value < 2.2e-16
# Medidas de tendencia y normalidad para la variable respuesta (price)
hist(listings$price, main="Distribución de Precios", xlab="Precio", col="coral", border="white")

boxplot(listings$price, main="Boxplot de Precios", col="coral")

lillie.test(sample(listings$price, min(5000, length(listings$price))))
## 
##  Lilliefors (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) normality test
## 
## data:  sample(listings$price, min(5000, length(listings$price)))
## D = 0.43335, p-value < 2.2e-16
mean(listings$bathrooms_num, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 1.611953
median(listings$bathrooms_num, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 1
min(listings$bathrooms_num, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 0
max(listings$bathrooms_num, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 20
quantile(listings$bathrooms_num, na.rm = TRUE)
##   0%  25%  50%  75% 100% 
##    0    1    1    2   20
hist(listings$bathrooms_num,
     main = "Histograma de Bathrooms",
     xlab = "Bathrooms",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "white")

boxplot(listings$bathrooms_num,
        main = "Boxplot de Bathrooms",
        col = "lightgreen")

lillie.test(sample(listings$bathrooms_num,
                   min(5000, length(listings$bathrooms_num))))
## 
##  Lilliefors (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) normality test
## 
## data:  sample(listings$bathrooms_num, min(5000, length(listings$bathrooms_num)))
## D = 0.29958, p-value < 2.2e-16
mean(listings$bedrooms, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 1.808252
median(listings$bedrooms, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 1
min(listings$bedrooms, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 0
max(listings$bedrooms, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 57
quantile(listings$bedrooms, na.rm = TRUE)
##   0%  25%  50%  75% 100% 
##    0    1    1    2   57
hist(listings$bedrooms,
     main = "Histograma de Bedrooms",
     xlab = "Bedrooms",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "white")

boxplot(listings$bedrooms,
        main = "Boxplot de Bedrooms",
        col = "lightgreen")

lillie.test(sample(listings$bedrooms,
                   min(5000, length(listings$bedrooms))))
## 
##  Lilliefors (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) normality test
## 
## data:  sample(listings$bedrooms, min(5000, length(listings$bedrooms)))
## D = 0.22826, p-value < 2.2e-16
mean(listings$beds, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 2.63682
median(listings$beds, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 2
min(listings$beds, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 0
max(listings$beds, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 132

##   0%  25%  50%  75% 100% 
##    0    1    2    3  132
hist(listings$beds,
     main = "Histograma de Beds",
     xlab = "Beds",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "white")

boxplot(listings$beds,
        main = "Boxplot de Beds",
        col = "lightgreen")

lillie.test(sample(listings$beds,
                   min(5000, length(listings$beds))))
## 
##  Lilliefors (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) normality test

                   min(5000, length(listings$availability_365))))
## 
##  Lilliefors (Kolmogorov-Smirnov) normality test
## 
## data:  sample(listings$availability_365, min(5000, length(listings$availability_365)))
## D = 0.10578, p-value < 2.2e-16
# A. Correlación de todas las variables numéricas (incluyendo el precio)
ggcorr(listings[, numericas_final], label = TRUE, label_size = 3, layout.exp = 1)

# B. Relación de variables numéricas vs Precio (Tu variable a predecir)
ggplot(listings, aes(x = accommodates, y = price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "blue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Relación entre Capacidad y Precio",
       x = "Capacidad (Accommodates)", y = "Precio")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# C. Relación categórica vs Precio 
ggplot(listings, aes(x = bathroom_type, y = price, fill = bathroom_type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Precio según tipo de baño")

# Fijar una semilla para que los resultados sean exactamente iguales cada vez que corras el código
set.seed(123)

# Crear un índice para dividir los datos: 80% entrenamiento, 20% prueba
indice <- sample(1:nrow(listings), size = 0.8 * nrow(listings))

# Separar los conjuntos
train_data <- listings[indice, ]
test_data <- listings[-indice, ]

cat("Filas para entrenar:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
## Filas para entrenar: 60996
cat("Filas para probar:", nrow(test_data), "\n")
## Filas para probar: 15250
# Generar el modelo: Precio explicado solo por la Capacidad
modelo_simple <- lm(price ~ accommodates, data = train_data)

# Ver el resumen estadístico
summary(modelo_simple)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = price ~ accommodates, data = train_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
##  -2292   -621   -426   -246  49673 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    81.760     32.586   2.509   0.0121 *  
## accommodates  138.628      5.722  24.228   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 4235 on 60994 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.009532,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.009516 
## F-statistic:   587 on 1 and 60994 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
# Gráficos de Residuos (ponemos par(mfrow=c(2,2)) para ver los 4 gráficos juntos)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(modelo_simple, main = "Residuos: Regresión Simple")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) # Regresamos la ventana a la normalidad
# Seleccionamos las variables numéricas y el tipo de baño categórico
variables_modelo <- c(numericas_final, "bathroom_type")

# Filtramos el dataset de entrenamiento para usar solo esas columnas
train_data_mod <- train_data[, variables_modelo]

# Generar el modelo: Precio explicado por TODAS las variables seleccionadas (el punto '.' significa 'todas')
modelo_multiple <- lm(price ~ ., data = train_data_mod)

# Ver el resumen estadístico
summary(modelo_multiple)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = price ~ ., data = train_data_mod)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
##  -9027   -908   -437     52  54341 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                      17.8996   315.3546   0.057  0.95474    
## accommodates                    493.1198    11.7147  42.094  < 2e-16 ***
## bathrooms_num                   -10.1858    27.5059  -0.370  0.71115    
## bedrooms                        129.9701    24.5183   5.301 1.16e-07 ***
## beds                           -727.7342    15.9210 -45.709  < 2e-16 ***
## host_response_rate                4.6124     1.6017   2.880  0.00398 ** 
## host_acceptance_rate              7.9193     0.8800   9.000  < 2e-16 ***
## calculated_host_listings_count    1.3773     0.1555   8.857  < 2e-16 ***
## number_of_reviews                -0.1691     0.2289  -0.739  0.46010    
## reviews_per_month              -246.4182    13.8573 -17.783  < 2e-16 ***
## review_scores_rating            -87.1567    57.5944  -1.513  0.13021    
## minimum_nights                   -7.9142     0.7709 -10.267  < 2e-16 ***
## availability_365                 -1.6098     0.1576 -10.213  < 2e-16 ***
## bathroom_typeshared             141.6676    73.9590   1.915  0.05544 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 4126 on 60982 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.05975,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.05954 
## F-statistic: 298.1 on 13 and 60982 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
# Gráficos de Residuos
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(modelo_multiple, main = "Residuos: Regresión Múltiple")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# Histograma extra para ver la distribución de los errores
hist(modelo_multiple$residuals, breaks = 50, col = "skyblue", border = "white",
     main = "Distribución de Residuos (Modelo Múltiple)", xlab = "Residuos")
